description: Analyse oligopoly pricing and output decisions using Cournot output competition, Nash equilibrium, Stackelberg leadership, kinked demand, and cartel models

fields.name: number_of_firms
fields.label: Number of firms in the oligopoly (typically 2 to 10)

fields.name: market_demand
fields.label: Market demand schedule or inverse demand function

cournot_model.definition: Firms choose output simultaneously, assuming rivals hold output fixed
cournot_model.equilibrium: Nash equilibrium in quantities; each firm's Q is a best response given rivals
cournot_model.duopoly_result: Each firm produces 1/3 of monopoly output (with identical constant MC)

nash_equilibrium.definition: Strategy profile in which no firm can improve profit by unilaterally changing strategy
nash_equilibrium.existence: Every finite game has at least one Nash equilibrium (possibly mixed)

stackelberg_model.definition: Sequential moves — a leader commits first, follower reacts
stackelberg_model.result: Leader gains first-mover advantage; total output higher than Cournot

kinked_demand.assumption: Rivals match price cuts but ignore price increases
kinked_demand.implication: Demand is elastic above and inelastic below the current price -> price stability

cartel.goal: Coordinate on joint monopoly output and price
cartel.challenge: Each member has incentive to cheat; stability depends on detection and punishment

bertrand_model.assumption: Firms compete on price with identical homogeneous products
bertrand_model.outcome: Price driven to marginal cost even with two firms (Bertrand paradox)

investment_applications.airline_routes: Capacity discipline vs price wars — Cournot dynamics
investment_applications.telecom: Oligopoly with Stackelberg leader in new-market entry
investment_applications.oil_market: OPEC+ cartel behaviour and compliance monitoring

validation.valid_model: model in {cournot, stackelberg, kinked_demand, cartel, bertrand}

simulate_oligopoly.description: Compute equilibrium outputs and prices for the chosen model

errors.message: model must be cournot, stackelberg, kinked_demand, cartel, or bertrand

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume oligopolies always reach the cartel outcome
anti_patterns.why: Cheating incentives and regulatory constraints often destabilise collusion; the actual outcome can be closer to Cournot or Bertrand

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply kinked-demand logic to industries with homogeneous products and transparent prices
anti_patterns.why: Bertrand or Cournot dynamics dominate; the kinked-demand model fits differentiated, non-transparent settings

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret the Nash equilibrium as the socially optimal outcome
anti_patterns.why: Nash is individually rational, not Pareto-efficient; prisoner's-dilemma situations illustrate this gap

anti_patterns.rule: Do not overlook antitrust risk when modelling cartel profits
anti_patterns.why: Regulators impose large fines and breakups; cartel NPV must net of enforcement risk

duopoly_output_ranking.monopoly: Q_m — lowest combined output, highest price
duopoly_output_ranking.stackelberg: Q_s > Q_cournot — leader forces larger total output
duopoly_output_ranking.cournot: Q_c — between Stackelberg and perfect competition
duopoly_output_ranking.bertrand: Q_pc — equal to perfect competition with P = MC
